label: Strategy Classification
definition: Categorization of an entity's business strategy

label: Viability Prediction
definition: Expert prediction of entity's or technology's long-term survival or adoption trajectory

label: Actual Outcome
definition: Verified historical outcome for an entity or technology, established post-study

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Evaluation of a technology's maturity, fitness for purpose, or competitive position

label: Benchmark Result
definition: Quantitative comparison against peers or standards; measured performance data

label: Framework Factor
definition: One component of a multi-factor analytical framework used by the study author

label: Market Data
definition: Quantitative market statistic such as size, share, growth rate, or revenue estimate

label: Expert Opinion
definition: Qualitative professional judgment or recommendation not tied to a specific quantitative metric

label: Industry Analysis
definition: Systematic analysis of industry structure, dynamics, competitive forces, and market trends

label: Competitive Profiling
definition: Detailed comparison of competing entities on key dimensions

label: Benchmarking
definition: Comparison against industry standards or best-in-class peers

label: Field Research
definition: Primary data collection via interviews, surveys, or direct observation

label: Document Review
definition: Analysis of existing documents, published reports, and records

label: Statistical Analysis
definition: Quantitative data analysis using statistical methods

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Structured evaluation of technology products and their fitness for enterprise use cases

label: Market Analysis
definition: Estimation and segmentation of market size, growth, and competitive structure

label: High Confidence
definition: Strong evidentiary basis; multiple corroborating sources or direct Aberdeen research finding

label: Medium Confidence
definition: Reasonable basis but limited corroboration; Aberdeen estimate or projection

label: Low Confidence
definition: Directional judgment with limited evidence; based on vendor statements or preliminary data

label: Verified
definition: Confirmed by subsequent events or independent sources; applied to actual-outcome observations

label: Emerging
definition: Technology was new and growing rapidly at time of study; limited market penetration

label: Mature
definition: Technology was established and widely adopted at time of study

label: Declining
definition: Technology was losing market share or relevance at time of study

label: Active
definition: Technology is currently actively maintained, developed, and commercially available

label: Legacy Supported
definition: Technology still in use but no longer actively developed; maintenance and support only

label: Obsolete
definition: Technology no longer in active use or commercial support; superseded by successor technologies

label: Store-and-Forward
definition: Messaging paradigm where data is stored at intermediate nodes until recipient is available; key to robust distributed computing

label: Enterprise Infrastructure
definition: Aberdeen Group concept: shared middleware foundation enabling location-independent distributed computing across all enterprise applications

label: Application Partitioning
definition: Second-generation CADE capability to automatically repartition application components between client and server tiers without user disruption

label: Second-Generation CADE
definition: Client-server application development environment with integrated Commercial Messaging, repository/directory, and application partitioning (e.g., Forte, Seer*HPS)

label: Queue Manager
definition: Core Commercial Messaging component handling queues, connections between Queue Managers, and message routing across the enterprise network
